methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Resolute Grid is a seed-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector. The comparable universe consists of nine peers across the sector: one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. This cohort spans multiple subsectors and funding stages within climate and energy infrastructure.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. A filtered peer set matching Resolute Grid's exact stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, falling below the minimum threshold for reliable median estimation. The broader Climate & Energy peer set of nine companies is too heterogeneous—spanning different technology areas, capital intensity profiles, and market maturity levels—to support a defensible same-stage median without introducing material bias.

Without a sufficient narrow comparable set, triangulation to a specific valuation range cannot be supported by the data provided. Any valuation estimate at this stage would require either: (1) expansion of the peer universe to identify additional seed-stage climate-tech companies with sufficient similarity, or (2) application of alternative methods (cost-to-date, venture capital method, or DCF) paired with explicit assumptions. The key uncertainty is the absence of stage-appropriate benchmarks; the broad sector universe alone is descriptive but not prescriptive for pricing.
